notes: Expert corrections based on domain knowledge of ECCO system capabilities. LLM analysis suffered from evidence contamination (extracting evidence from citing papers about OTHER models like ADCIRC, CICE standalone, etc.)

MCL-1.reason: A stable, global multi-decadal ocean model requires sophisticated representation of salient ocean physical processes. MITgcm includes comprehensive ocean physics, KPP mixing, sea ice thermodynamics/dynamics, and optional biogeochemistry.
evidence: Full primitive equation ocean dynamics
KPP vertical mixing scheme
Sea ice thermodynamics and dynamics
Nonlinear free surface
Partial cell bottom topography for accurate bathymetry

MCL-2.reason: ECCO/MITgcm operates across a wide range of spatial scales. The LLC4320 simulation demonstrates global submesoscale-resolving capability at 1/48 degree (~2km). ECCO products range from 1 degree to O(100m) resolution.
evidence: ECCO Version 4: 1 degree global configuration
LLC4320: 1/48 degree global ocean with tides (~2km)
Regional configurations down to O(100m) resolution
Lat-Lon-Cap grid enables efficient global coverage
One of ~3-4 codes capable of global O(100m) resolution

MCL-3.reason: ECCO products span hourly output to multi-decadal reanalysis (1992-present, 30+ years). The LLC4320 produces hourly output capturing tidal and sub-daily variability.
evidence: Hourly output from LLC4320 (tides, diurnal cycles)
Daily to monthly mean products
Multi-decadal continuous reanalysis (1992-present)
Consistent physics across all time scales
Sub-daily to decadal variability captured

MCL-4.reason: ECCO has 4 coupled components: ocean circulation, sea ice (thermodynamic+dynamic), biogeochemistry, and thermodynamic ice sheets. This is clearly HIGH (3-4 components). VERY_HIGH would require 5+ components or full ESM coupling.
evidence: Ocean circulation (MITgcm core)
Sea ice model with thermodynamic and dynamic components
Ocean biogeochemistry (Darwin ecosystem model integration)
Thermodynamic ice sheet coupling for ice shelf/ocean interaction

MCL-5.reason: ECCO is a retrospective state estimation / reanalysis system and high-resolution nature run facility. It is NOT a prediction system. Predictive skill is not applicable.
evidence: ECCO produces retrospective ocean state estimates constrained by observations
High-resolution 'nature runs' are simulations, not forecasts
System is designed for historical reconstruction, not prediction

MCL-6.reason: MITgcm (ECCO's underlying model) is one of only ~3-4 ocean model codes worldwide capable of running globally at O(100m) resolution. This represents world-class computational performance.
evidence: MITgcm is one of few codes capable of global O(100m) ocean resolution
Demonstrated scaling to hundreds of thousands of cores
LLC4320 simulation: 1/48 degree global ocean with tides
Adjoint model capability for large-scale optimization

MCL-7.reason: ECCO's core mission is multi-source observational constraint. The system assimilates satellite altimetry, SST, SSS, GRACE gravity, Argo profiles, XBTs, CTDs, moorings, and more - approximately 10^9 observations.
evidence: Satellite altimetry (Jason, Sentinel, etc.)
Sea surface temperature (multiple sources)
Sea surface salinity (Aquarius, SMOS, SMAP)
GRACE/GRACE-FO ocean bottom pressure
Argo float profiles (~2 million profiles)
Historical XBT, CTD, mooring data
Sea ice concentration and thickness

MCL-10.reason: ECCO involves continuous V&V against approximately 10^9 observations through its data assimilation framework. The optimization process IS verification/validation.
evidence: Continuous validation against ~10^9 observations in state estimation
Formal cost function minimization quantifies model-data misfit
Participation in ocean model intercomparison projects (OMIP)
Systematic comparison with independent datasets not in assimilation

MCL-8.reason: Retrospective ocean state estimation IS ECCO's core mission. ECCO produces coupled, fully integrated, benchmarked reanalysis products spanning 1992-present (30+ years) using 4D-Var adjoint method.
evidence: ECCO Version 4 Release 4: 1992-2017 global ocean state estimate
ECCO Version 5: Extended and updated reanalysis
4D-Var adjoint method for optimal state estimation
Fully dynamically consistent solutions (no nudging artifacts)
Benchmarked against MERRA-2, ERA5, and other reanalyses

MCL-13.reason: ECCO model codes (MITgcm), forcing fields, and analysis routines are 100% open source. All official ECCO releases adhere to CF and ACDD conventions for interoperability, as mandated by NASA ESDS.
evidence: MITgcm is fully open source (MIT license) on GitHub
ECCO forcing fields publicly available
Analysis routines (ecco_v4_py) open source
Official releases CF/ACDD compliant per NASA ESDS mandate
Active user community and documentation

notes_on_llm_evidence_contamination.MCL-1: Evidence item 1 ('ADCIRC+SWAN') is NOT about ECCO - it's a different model that may use ECCO boundary conditions
notes_on_llm_evidence_contamination.MCL-6: Evidence items reference generic HPC usage, not ECCO's specific world-class performance capabilities
notes_on_llm_evidence_contamination.MCL-10: Evidence items 1-3 ('CORA', 'SOCAT', 'DASSO') are NOT about ECCO validation - they are other systems
notes_on_llm_evidence_contamination.MCL-13: Evidence items 1,2,5 ('ADCIRC+SWAN', 'CICE standalone', 'CESM') are NOT about ECCO's open science status
